framework_selection.config_system: YAML + dataclass (no Hydra dependency; plain PyYAML + argparse)
framework_selection.reasoning: Paper trains on TPU/TF but all major reproductions target PyTorch+GPU. HuggingFace Datasets handles C4/GLUE/SQuAD access cleanly. HuggingFace Tokenizers provides the SentencePiece 32K tokenizer equivalent. Custom ops (SGU, Toeplitz) are expressible in pure PyTorch without C++ extensions.
framework_selection.precision: torch.float32 default; bfloat16 optional via --precision bf16 flag (matches original TPU setup)

gmlp/.__init__.py: Package root, exports gMLP, aMLP, gMLPConfig

models/.gmlp.py: gMLP top-level model (NLP and Vision variants)
models/.toeplitz.py: ToeplitzLinear — parameterizes W as 2n-1 vector with circulant construction

data/.mlm_dataset.py: MLMDataset — wraps HuggingFace C4, applies BERT-style 15% masking
data/.glue_dataset.py: GLUEDataset — SST-2 / MNLI via HuggingFace datasets
data/.transforms.py: AutoAugment, Mixup, CutMix, CutMixMixupSwitch for vision

training/.trainer_nlp.py: NLPTrainer — MLM pretraining loop; AdamW, linear LR, gradient clipping
training/.trainer_vision.py: VisionTrainer — ImageNet loop; AdamW, cosine LR, stochastic depth control
training/.finetuner.py: NLPFinetuner — SST-2/MNLI classification head + SQuAD QA head
training/.losses.py: MLMLoss (cross-entropy on masked tokens), ClassificationLoss, QALoss (span extraction)

evaluation/.evaluator.py: Evaluator class — wraps metrics, handles median-of-N-runs aggregation

utils/.config.py: gMLPConfig dataclass — all model + training hyperparameters with defaults

data/.download_c4.sh: HuggingFace datasets download script for C4
data/.download_imagenet.sh: ImageNet download and validation split setup

classes.name: ToeplitzLinear
classes.description: Parameterizes spatial weight matrix W as a single vector w ∈ R^{2n-1}. At forward time constructs the full [n,n] Toeplitz matrix via circulant padding+reshape. Optionally falls back to unconstrained [n,n] parameter (for vision).

public_methods.forward: (z: Tensor[B,n,e]) -> Tensor[B,n,e]  — apply W @ z + b along spatial dim

classes.init_strategy: w init near-zero (std=0.002); b init ones
classes.note: W std value is assumed (ambiguity_002). Implement with configurable std parameter.

classes.name: TinyAttention
classes.description: Single-head scaled dot-product attention. Takes pre-block normalized input X (not the split Z2). Projects to QKV of size d_attn each, computes attention, projects output back to d_out.
classes.init_args: d_model: int, d_attn: int=64, d_out: int=None

classes.name: SpatialGatingUnit
classes.description: Core gMLP spatial interaction module. Splits input Z into Z1 (gate bypass) and Z2 (spatial branch). Applies LayerNorm → ToeplitzLinear (or dense) → elementwise multiply with Z1.
classes.init_args: d_ffn: int, seq_len: int, use_toeplitz: bool=True, w_init_std: float=0.002
forward_steps: z1, z2 = split(z, d_ffn//2, dim=-1)   # [B,n,d_ffn/2] each

classes.name: aMLP_SGU
classes.description: Hybrid SGU for aMLP. Spatial gate = ToeplitzLinear(Z2) + TinyAttention(X_pre). Uses configurable fusion_mode ('add' default per ambiguity_001 resolution).
classes.init_args: d_ffn: int, d_model: int, seq_len: int, d_attn: int=64, fusion_mode: str='add'
forward_steps: attn_gate = tiny_attn(x_pre)             # [B,n,d_ffn/2]  ← x_pre is pre-block norm output
gate = spatial_gate + attn_gate           # fusion_mode='add' (TODO: verify from paper)
classes.note: fusion_mode should be configurable; 'add' is primary assumption per ambiguity_001

classes.name: gMLPBlock
classes.description: One gMLP residual block. LayerNorm → expand → GeLU → SGU (or aMLP_SGU) → contract → residual add. Used for both NLP and vision.
classes.init_args: d_model: int, d_ffn: int, seq_len: int, use_toeplitz: bool=True, use_tiny_attn: bool=False, d_attn: int=64, w_init_std: float=0.002, survival_prob: float=1.0
forward_steps: x = stochastic_depth(x, survival_prob)        # training only, vision only

classes.name: PatchEmbedding
classes.description: ViT-style 16×16 non-overlapping patch embedding. Conv2d(3, d_model, kernel=16, stride=16) → flatten spatial dims → [B, num_patches, d_model]. No positional encoding added.
classes.init_args: img_size: int=224, patch_size: int=16, in_channels: int=3, d_model: int

classes.name: gMLP
classes.description: Top-level model. Dispatches to NLP or Vision mode. NLP: token embedding → L × gMLPBlock → LM head or CLS head. Vision: PatchEmbedding → L × gMLPBlock → global avg pool → classifier.

public_methods.forward: (input_ids: Tensor[B,n] OR pixel_values: Tensor[B,3,H,W], attention_mask: Optional[Tensor]=None, labels: Optional[Tensor]=None) -> ModelOutput

classes.note_pooling: Global avg pool assumed per ambiguity_003 (confidence 0.70). Implement with config flag allow_cls_pool=True as fallback.

classes.name: gMLPConfig
classes.description: Central dataclass holding all model and training hyperparameters. Serializable to/from YAML.

tensor_flows.name: gMLP NLP Forward (MLM pretraining)
steps: x = embed(input_ids):  [B, n, d_model]  float32      ← token embedding table
x = x_pre @ U:    [B, n, d_ffn]                   ← channel expand (U∈[d_model,d_ffn])
z2 = W@z2+b:      [B, n, d_ffn/2]                 ← Toeplitz W∈[n,n], b∈[n]
x = z1 * z2:      [B, n, d_ffn/2]                 ← SGU output
x = x @ V:        [B, n, d_model]                 ← channel contract (V∈[d_ffn/2,d_model])
logits = x @ E.T:   [B, n, vocab_size]              ← weight-tied LM head
loss = CE(logits[mask], labels[mask])               ← only on masked positions

tensor_flows.name: aMLP NLP Forward (adds tiny attention)
tensor_flows.patch: Inside each block, after z1,z2 = split(x):
additional_steps: spatial_gate = W@z2+b:  [B, n, d_ffn/2]          ← same as gMLP
q,k,v = x_pre @ Wqkv:  [B, n, d_attn] each       ← project pre-norm input
attn_gate = attn_out @ Wo: [B, n, d_ffn/2]        ← project to gate size

tensor_flows.name: gMLP Vision Forward (ImageNet classification)
steps: (same block structure as NLP but W∈R^{196×196}, NOT Toeplitz)
x = x.mean(dim=1):  [B, d_model]                   ← global average pool (ambiguity_003)

model.w_init_std: 0.002  # ASSUMED: paper says near-zero, std not specified (ambiguity_002, conf=0.65)
model.attn_fusion_mode: add  # ASSUMED: additive fusion per Fig6 (ambiguity_001, conf=0.75)

model.pool_mode: avg  # ASSUMED: global avg pool (ambiguity_003, conf=0.70)

rationale.accelerate: Multi-GPU / mixed precision training without boilerplate
rationale.einops: Clean tensor rearrangement for Toeplitz construction and patch ops
rationale.timm: AutoAugment policy implementation for ImageNet training
rationale.sentencepiece: Required by BERT tokenizer used in paper

entrypoints.purpose: NLP MLM pretraining (gMLP or aMLP on C4)
NLP finetuning on GLUE / SQuAD from a pretrained checkpoint
Evaluate a checkpoint on any supported task
Single-sample demo: masked token prediction (NLP) or class prediction (vision)

risk_assessment.description: C4 (full English) is ~300GB compressed. Download and preprocessing time is significant. HuggingFace streaming mode should be enabled to avoid full disk materialization.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use datasets.load_dataset('c4', 'en', streaming=True). Provide fallback to C4/RealNews (smaller subset) for ablation reproduction.

risk_assessment.description: Full BERTbase-equivalent training (1M steps, 256 batch, 512 seq_len) requires ~100+ A100-GPU-hours. gMLPbase is larger (130M params, higher FLOPs) so even more.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Provide ablation-scale config (125K steps, 128 seq_len, 2048 batch, C4/RealNews) as the default runnable config. Full training is optional.

risk_assessment.description: aMLP fusion mechanism (how tiny_attn combines with spatial gate) has confidence 0.75. Wrong fusion could cause significant performance regression on MNLI/SQuAD.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement fusion_mode as a config parameter ('add', 'concat', 'replace'). Default 'add'. Document as TODO:verify.

risk_assessment.description: W initialization std is unspecified. Too large → SGU diverges early (bypasses FFN-like initialization goal). Too small → no learning in spatial weights.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default w_init_std=0.002. Add numerical stability check (monitor SGU gate norms in first 1K steps). Make configurable.

risk_assessment.description: The spatial projection W ∈ R^{n×n} is quadratic in sequence length. At n=512 (BERT setup), W has 262K parameters per layer × 48 layers = 12.6M spatial params. Memory and compute are O(n²) per layer.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Enforce Toeplitz constraint for NLP (reduces to 2n-1 params per layer). Warn user when n > 512. The Toeplitz trick is quality-neutral per the paper.

risk_assessment.description: Whether CLS token or global average pool is used for vision classification has confidence 0.70.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement both; default global avg pool (pool_mode='avg'). Add config flag pool_mode='cls' as alternative.

risk_assessment.description: Paper uses 32K cased SentencePiece vocabulary. HuggingFace BertTokenizer uses WordPiece. For exact reproduction, sentencepiece model must be trained on C4 or sourced from T5/mT5.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use google/t5-base tokenizer (SentencePiece, 32K vocab, cased) as a close proxy. Document the substitution.

risk_assessment.description: Stochastic depth survival_prob per layer is specified only for the 3 vision model sizes. NLP models do not use it.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement as DropPath per block; set p=0.0 for NLP configs. Vision configs use values from Table 1.
